METRO MATCH

DECK OF 18

Stat cards for the world’s great metro systems, every number dated and sourced. Flip a card for the lore; pick a stat and beat the cpu; one guess a day in the daily.

STATS
tokyo
13 lines · Metro + Toei
01/18
5thopened1927
8thstations303
15thspan32.6 km
4thdensity0.71 st/km²
9throute-km304 km
3rdridership3.46 B/yr
12thbase fare$1.12
12thdriverless0 lines
4thinterchange35 %
11thnew lines0 since ’16
13thnewest line2008
data: OSM snapshot 2026-06-12 (ODbL) + dated almanac · base fare at 2026-06-12 FX · ranks across the deck of 18
tokyo
the two crews
Thirteen lines, two operators, one grid.
Two operators share one grid: Tokyo Metro runs nine lines, Toei runs four.
G is for Ginza: Asia’s first metro line, opened 1927.
“Tokyo Subway Linemap” by Yveltal, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0
seoul
24 lines · capital region network
02/18
10thopened1974
1ststations730
1stspan146.1 km
18thdensity0.069 st/km²
1stroute-km1,302 km
1stridership4.42 B/yr
10thbase fare$1.02
2nddriverless7 lines
12thinterchange22 %
3rdnew lines7 since ’16
2ndnewest line2024
data: OSM snapshot 2026-06-12 (ODbL) + dated almanac · base fare at 2026-06-12 FX · ranks across the deck of 18
seoul
the sprawl
146 km between its furthest stations, the deck's longest reach.
Line 2 is a 48 km loop around the city; trains circle it all day.
Every station on the network has platform screen doors; the rollout finished by 2017.
“Seoul Metropolitan Subway network map” by Satellizer, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0
diagram dated 2023, future lines as then planned
singapore
6 lines · MRT only
03/18
12thopened1987
13thstations176
9thspan39.4 km
7thdensity0.37 st/km²
13throute-km243 km
12thridership1.25 B/yr
9thbase fare$0.99
4thdriverless6 lines
10thinterchange24 %
10thnew lines1 since ’16
7thnewest line2020
data: OSM snapshot 2026-06-12 (ODbL) + dated almanac · base fare at 2026-06-12 FX · ranks across the deck of 18
singapore
the clockwork
Six lines, four of them driverless, none older than 1987.
Many MRT stations double as civil defence shelters, built blast resistant.
The North East Line opened in 2003 as one of the world’s first fully automated heavy metro lines.
“Singapore MRT and LRT System Map” by Aforl, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 3.0
hong kong
10 lines · MTR rail, light rail out
04/18
11thopened1979
16thstations105
12thspan34.8 km
14thdensity0.14 st/km²
14throute-km209 km
8thridership1.71 B/yr
7thbase fare$0.51
10thdriverless2 lines
11thinterchange23 %
8thnew lines2 since ’16
7thnewest line2020
data: OSM snapshot 2026-06-12 (ODbL) + dated almanac · base fare at 2026-06-12 FX · ranks across the deck of 18
hong kong
the farebox
Fares cover the costs and then some: the system runs at a profit.
The rail plus property model funds new lines with real estate built above the stations.
The East Rail corridor dates to 1910, nearly seventy years older than the MTR itself.
“Hong Kong Railway Route Map” by Sameboat, Wikimedia Commons, public domain
paris
16 lines · full Métro
05/18
2ndopened1900
5thstations389
17thspan22.2 km
1stdensity1.35 st/km²
12throute-km246 km
9thridership1.48 B/yr

5thinterchange34 %
11thnew lines0 since ’16
16thnewest line1998
data: OSM snapshot 2026-06-12 (ODbL) + dated almanac · base fare at 2026-06-12 FX · ranks across the deck of 18
paris
the mesh
1.35 stations per square km, the deck's tightest grid.
Opened for the 1900 World’s Fair; Line 1 still runs the route it opened with.
86 of Guimard’s Art Nouveau entrances from the early 1900s still stand.
“Carte Métro de Paris” by Rigil, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY 3.0
shanghai
19 lines · metro proper, maglev out
06/18
15thopened1993
3rdstations429
3rdspan93.7 km
15thdensity0.13 st/km²
3rdroute-km816 km

5thbase fare$0.44
2nddriverless7 lines
13thinterchange21 %
4thnew lines5 since ’16
6thnewest line2021
data: OSM snapshot 2026-06-12 (ODbL) + dated almanac · base fare at 2026-06-12 FX · ranks across the deck of 18
shanghai
the giant
From first train to 800 route-km inside three decades.
Set a one-day record of 13.39 million rides on 9 March 2024.
Five lines run driverless, about 169 km of unattended metro.
“Shanghai Metro Linemap” by Yveltal, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0
beijing
27 lines · full mapped network
07/18
8thopened1969
4thstations407
4thspan83.0 km
16thdensity0.12 st/km²
2ndroute-km909 km

5thbase fare$0.44
1stdriverless8 lines
6thinterchange26 %
1stnew lines10 since ’16
2ndnewest line2024
data: OSM snapshot 2026-06-12 (ODbL) + dated almanac · base fare at 2026-06-12 FX · ranks across the deck of 18
beijing
the heavyweight
909 route-km under one operator, the deck's biggest single system.
China’s first subway, built as a Cold War defence project; Zhou Enlai rode the first train in 1969.
Early tickets required an introduction letter from your work unit.
“Beijing Subway System Map” by Painjet, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0
london
11 lines · Underground only
08/18
1stopened1863
7thstations323
6thspan61.9 km
10thdensity0.23 st/km²

2ndinterchange36 %
11thnew lines0 since ’16
17thnewest line1979
data: OSM snapshot 2026-06-12 (ODbL) + dated almanac · base fare at 2026-06-12 FX · ranks across the deck of 18
london
the original
Underground since 1863, before anyone else tried.
Despite the name, only about 45 percent of the network is below ground.
Tube stations doubled as air-raid shelters during the Blitz.
“London Underground Overground DLR Crossrail map” by Sameboat, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0
new york
23 services · subway services, S as one
09/18
3rdopened1904
2ndstations504
10thspan37.7 km
2nddensity0.87 st/km²
7throute-km399 km
11thridership1.30 B/yr
17thbase fare$3.00
12thdriverless0 lines
1stinterchange65 %
11thnew lines0 since ’16
18thnewest line1932
data: OSM snapshot 2026-06-12 (ODbL) + dated almanac · base fare at 2026-06-12 FX · ranks across the deck of 18
new york
the all-nighter
24 hours a day, every day of the year.
Express tracks let fast trains overtake locals, a luxury most metros never built.
Its 472 stations are the most of any single-operator metro in the world.
“NYC subway map” by CountZ (Jake Berman) & TFSyndicate, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 3.0
the Tauranac-form diagram, the city’s map for 45 years
madrid
13 lines · Metro only, ML out
10/18

9thstations295

5thdensity0.46 st/km²

8thinterchange25 %
11thnew lines0 since ’16
15thnewest line2003
data: OSM snapshot 2026-06-12 (ODbL) + dated almanac · base fare at 2026-06-12 FX · ranks across the deck of 18
madrid
the dark horse
172 km of new metro in sixteen years, Europe's great expansion.
Between 1995 and 2011 the network added 172 km and 132 stations, over 10 km a year.
Chamberí station, bypassed in the 1960s, survives as a time-capsule museum of the 1919 metro.
“Madrid Metro Map” by Javitomad and contributors, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0
moscow
16 lines · metro + MCC, MCD out
11/18
7thopened1935
11thstations267
8thspan42.5 km
9thdensity0.25 st/km²
5throute-km535 km
7thridership2.31 B/yr
11thbase fare$1.04
12thdriverless0 lines
6thinterchange26 %
6thnew lines4 since ’16
2ndnewest line2024
data: OSM snapshot 2026-06-12 (ODbL) + dated almanac · base fare at 2026-06-12 FX · ranks across the deck of 18
moscow
the palace
Marble, mosaics and a train every 90 seconds.
The Stalin-era stations were built as palaces for the people, in marble and chandeliers.
In 2023 Moscow cut rush-hour intervals to 80 seconds, a world first.
“Moscow metro map” by Sameboat & IKhitron, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0
copenhagen
4 lines · metro M1-M4
12/18
17thopened2002
18thstations45
18thspan11.6 km
3rddensity0.75 st/km²
18throute-km43 km
18thridership135 M/yr
18thbase fare$3.71
5thdriverless4 lines
2ndinterchange36 %
8thnew lines2 since ’16
7thnewest line2020
data: OSM snapshot 2026-06-12 (ODbL) + dated almanac · base fare at 2026-06-12 FX · ranks across the deck of 18
copenhagen
the robot
Four lines, zero drivers, all night.
Every train has run driverless since the first one in 2002.
One of only three metros in the world running 24/7, with New York and the Chicago L.
“Copenhagen Metro 2024” by Tomtom24, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0
delhi
9 lines · DMRC + airport line
13/18
17thopened2002
10thstations270
7thspan55.4 km
11thdensity0.21 st/km²
8throute-km374 km
6thridership2.36 B/yr
1stbase fare$0.11

16thinterchange11 %
7thnew lines3 since ’16
10thnewest line2019
data: OSM snapshot 2026-06-12 (ODbL) + dated almanac · base fare at 2026-06-12 FX · ranks across the deck of 18
delhi
the speedrun
374 route-km in barely two decades, and it pays its own way.
The first metro in the world to earn UN carbon credits, in 2011.
India’s first driverless trains entered service on the Magenta Line in 2020.
“Delhi Metro Network 2020” by AshuArtsNew, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0
diagram dated 2020; Phase-4 openings not yet drawn
guangzhou
19 lines · GZ metro + Guangfo + APM
14/18
16thopened1997
6thstations325
2ndspan96.1 km
17thdensity0.073 st/km²
4throute-km780 km

5thdriverless4 lines
8thinterchange25 %
1stnew lines10 since ’16
1stnewest line2025
data: OSM snapshot 2026-06-12 (ODbL) + dated almanac · base fare at 2026-06-12 FX · ranks across the deck of 18
guangzhou
the workhorse
From a 5 km starter line in 1997 to 780 km today.
Lines 18 and 22 run at 160 km/h, the fastest metro service in China.
The Guangfo line, opened 2010, was China’s first intercity metro.
“Guangzhou Metro Network” by Alan Fan Pei, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0
mexico city
12 lines · STC metro
15/18
8thopened1969
12thstations195
16thspan31.1 km

11thnewest line2012
data: OSM snapshot 2026-06-12 (ODbL) + dated almanac · base fare at 2026-06-12 FX · ranks across the deck of 18
mexico city
the icons
A pictogram for every station, a 5-peso fare for every ride.
Every station has its own pictogram, a 1969 design for navigating without reading.
Pino Suárez station holds a small Aztec temple uncovered during construction.
“Plano del Metro de la Ciudad de México” by ManuelContreras1996, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0
diagram dated 2023, drawn to the sections then in service
cairo
3 lines · metro lines 1-3
16/18
12thopened1987
17thstations84

11thnewest line2012
data: OSM snapshot 2026-06-12 (ODbL) + dated almanac · base fare at 2026-06-12 FX · ranks across the deck of 18
cairo
the pioneer
Africa’s first metro, opened 1987, still its workhorse.
Africa’s and the Middle East’s first metro, opened 1987.
Trains have carried women-only cars since 1989.
“Cairo Metro map” by Momooh, Wikimedia Commons, CC0
osaka
9 lines · Osaka Metro, JR/monorail out
17/18
6thopened1933
15thstations135
14thspan34.1 km

14thnewest line2006
data: OSM snapshot 2026-06-12 (ODbL) + dated almanac · base fare at 2026-06-12 FX · ranks across the deck of 18
osaka
the merchant
Japan’s oldest municipal subway, anchored by the packed Midosuji spine.
The Midosuji Line opened in 1933 as Japan’s first municipally run subway.
The New Tram has run as a driverless people-mover since 1981.
“Wide-Area Map of Osaka City Subway” by Excl-zoo, Wikimedia Commons, public domain
diagram c.2011 in the pre-2018 Municipal Subway idiom; the 2024 and 2025 extensions not drawn
istanbul
10 lines · M-lines, Marmaray out
18/18
14thopened1989
14thstations159
5thspan63.4 km
13thdensity0.17 st/km²
11throute-km248 km
17thridership613 M/yr
8thbase fare$0.87
5thdriverless4 lines
17thinterchange9 %
4thnew lines5 since ’16
5thnewest line2023
data: OSM snapshot 2026-06-12 (ODbL) + dated almanac · base fare at 2026-06-12 FX · ranks across the deck of 18
istanbul
the crossing
A metro on two continents, and one of the world’s fastest-growing.
The M5, opened 2017, was Turkey’s first driverless metro line.
Its lines run on both the European and Asian sides of the Bosphorus.
“Istanbul Railway Systems Network Map” by Alikam1896, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0

ROUND 1
pick the stat you think wins
you 0 · cpu 0 · first to 3
pick the stat you think beats the hidden card · a dead heat scores nobody

THE DAILY ·
Which plots more stations?
vs
the stat rotates daily · the streak lives in this browser

The game

The battle. You hold a card face up; the cpu holds one face down. Pick the stat you think wins; the cards compare, and the round goes to the better number. First to three rounds takes the match. A dead heat scores nobody. The battle runs on the PLAY set.

The daily. One question a day, one guess, and a streak that lives in your browser. Nothing leaves the page.

Win directions are fixed. Opened and base fare win smaller; everything else wins larger.

PLAY: the six

statwhat it measureswins
openedearliest regular passenger service within the card’s declared scope, the system’s own dating, from operator historiesearlier
stationsstation complexes plotted from the frozen snapshot: named stations, same-name platforms merged within 350 m, interchanges counted oncemore
spanthe furthest-stations distance: the geodesic between the two plotted stations farthest apartmore
densitystations per square km of network extent, the convex hull of the plotted stationsmore
route-kmreported route length, dated and sourced (almanac grade)more
ridershipreported annual rides, dated and sourced (almanac grade)more

ALMANAC: the three

A second switchable set. Every figure is dated; sources are in the almanac file in the site’s public repo.

statwhat it measureswins
base farethe adult single minimum fare, converted to USD at one dated FX snapshot (2026-06-12) for every card, so the row compares on one rate; check the operator for today’s farecheaper
driverlesscount of GoA3+ lines (driverless, attended or not) within the card’s declared scope, from Wikipedia’s driverless-train-systems listmore
interchangeshare of the card’s station complexes whose plotted points sit by two or more counted lines, computed from our own snapshotmore

Two notes on interchange: it is computed from the same plotted snapshot as the other geometry stats, and New York reads high because its lettered and numbered services share track through most stations, so a single complex counts many services. The cut stats (peak headway, service hours, farebox recovery) were dropped for want of one comparable, current source across the deck.

Scope: what each card counts

Each card declares its scope as the network its city’s own familiar map draws as coequal metro lines: the rider’s network, not one operator’s books. Modes the map itself marks as distinct products (commuter overlays, trams, feeders, people-movers) stay out. Frozen per city 2026-06-12 (D25):

tokyoTokyo Metro + Toei only; through-running truncated at the scope boundary (the familiar Tokyo Subway map stops there).
seoulThe full capital-region network the familiar map draws, incl. Line 1's long Korail corridors, the K-lines, GTX-A, AREX, the light metros and Incheon 1-2. One snapshot gap: Sinbundang is in the declared scope but its OSM validator export is empty, so plotted counts omit it until the snapshot heals.
singaporeMRT lines only; the LRT feeders are a distinct product on the LTA map.
hong kongMTR heavy rail incl. the Airport Express and the Disneyland Resort line; Light Rail is the map's district inset, not the metro.
parisMétro 1-14 plus 3bis and 7bis; RER excluded (it is not the Métro map).
shanghaiMetro lines 1-18 plus the Pujiang line; the maglev is a separate product even on the official map.
beijingThe operator's full mapped network: 19 numbered lines, the named suburban lines, S1, Xijiao and both airport expresses; Batong rides as Line 1 and Daxing as Line 4, as the map draws them.
londonUnderground's 11 lines only; the Elizabeth line, Overground and DLR are distinct products in the Tube map's own grammar.
new yorkSubway services only; rush-hour diamond variants fold into their base service, the three S shuttles ride as one pill, and Staten Island Railway and PATH are separate systems.
madridMetro de Madrid lines 1-12 plus the Ramal; Metro Ligero is its own product on the map.
moscowMetro lines incl. the MCC ring (the map's line 14); the D-branded MCD diameters are commuter rail, excluded.
copenhagenMetro M1-M4 only; S-tog is a distinct product.
delhiThe DMRC network incl. the Airport Express; Rapid Metro Gurgaon and the Aqua line are separate concessions.
guangzhouGuangzhou Metro incl. the Guangfo through-line and the APM; Foshan Metro's own lines and the trams are distinct systems.
mexico cityThe STC Metro's 12 lines; Tren Ligero and the Suburbano are separate systems.
cairoMetro lines 1-3; the LRT and the monorail are separate systems.
osakaOsaka Metro's nine lines incl. the New Tram (a coequal line on the map); the JR Loop and the Osaka Monorail are distinct products. Midosuji's through-run to Minoo-kayano is drawn continuous, so it stays; Kintetsu and Hankyu through-running truncates at the boundary.
istanbulThe branded Metro Istanbul M-lines (M1A and M1B fold to M1). Marmaray is TCDD commuter rail, excluded like Moscow's MCD; the funiculars and trams are separate feeder products.

Sources and dates

Geometry, station counts, span and density: OpenStreetMap via the subway preprocessor CDN, snapshot 2026-06-12, ODbL. The page is built offline from committed snapshots; raw GeoJSON never ships.

Opened years: operator histories, per-line, dated (sources in the almanac file in the site’s public repo). Reported figures:

cityroute-kmannual rides
tokyo304 km as of 2026 jametro.or.jp3,460 M FY2024 tokyometro.jp
seoul1,302 km as of 2026 en.wikipedia.org4,420 M 2024 railstat.korail.com
singapore243 km as of 2026 en.wikipedia.org1,249 M 2024 lta.gov.sg
hong kong209 km as of 2022-12 en.wikipedia.org1,714 M 2025 mtr.com.hk
paris246 km as of 2024 en.wikipedia.org1,476 M 2024 en.wikipedia.org
shanghai816 km as of 2026 en.wikipedia.org3,661 M 2023 en.wikipedia.org
beijing909 km as of 2026-06 en.wikipedia.org3,450 M 2023 en.wikipedia.org
london402 km as of 2026 en.wikipedia.org1,181 M 2023/24 en.wikipedia.org
new york399 km as of 2026 en.wikipedia.org1,300 M 2025 governor.ny.gov
madrid297 km as of 2025-09 en.wikipedia.org715 M 2024 comunidad.madrid
moscow535 km as of 2026-01 en.wikipedia.org2,313 M 2025 en.wikipedia.org
copenhagen43 km as of 2026 en.wikipedia.org135 M 2025 metroselskabet.dk
delhi374 km as of 2026-03-08 en.wikipedia.org2,358 M 2025 psuconnect.in
guangzhou780 km as of 2026-05-07 zh.wikipedia.org3,399 M 2025 m.21jingji.com
mexico city201 km as of 2026 en.wikipedia.org1,242 M 2025 cronica.com.mx
cairo107 km as of 2024-05-15 en.wikipedia.org1,460 M 2023 en.wikipedia.org
osaka141 km as of 2026 en.wikipedia.org912 M 2025 en.wikipedia.org
istanbul248 km as of 2026 en.wikipedia.org613 M 2023 en.wikipedia.org
almanac set: figures and sources
citybase fare (USD, 2026-06-12 FX)driverless (GoA3+)
tokyo1.12 USD 2026 gotokyo.org0 lines 2026-06-08 (oldid 1358345659) en.wikipedia.org
seoul1.02 USD 2025-06-28 koreaherald.com7 lines 2026-06-08 (oldid 1358345659) en.wikipedia.org
singapore0.99 USD 2026-01-28 ptc.gov.sg6 lines 2026-06-08 (oldid 1358345659) en.wikipedia.org
hong kong0.51 USD 2026 opendata.mtr.com.hk2 lines 2026-06-08 (oldid 1358345659) en.wikipedia.org
paris2.36 USD 2026-01-01 service-public.gouv.fr3 lines 2026-06-08 (oldid 1358345659) en.wikipedia.org
shanghai0.44 USD 2026 en.wikipedia.org7 lines 2026-06-08 (oldid 1358345659) en.wikipedia.org
beijing0.44 USD 2026-06 travelchinaguide.com8 lines 2026-06-08 (oldid 1358345659) en.wikipedia.org
london2.94 USD 2026-03-01 timeout.com0 lines 2026-06-08 (oldid 1358345659) en.wikipedia.org
new york3 USD 2026-01 ny1.com0 lines 2026-06-08 (oldid 1358345659) en.wikipedia.org
madrid1.73 USD 2026 moncloa.com0 lines 2026-06-08 (oldid 1358345659) en.wikipedia.org
moscow1.04 USD 2026-01 msk1.ru0 lines 2026-06-08 (oldid 1358345659) en.wikipedia.org
copenhagen3.71 USD 2026 publictransport.dk4 lines 2026-06-08 (oldid 1358345659) en.wikipedia.org
delhi0.11 USD 2025-08-25 theweek.in3 lines 2026-06-08 (oldid 1358345659) en.wikipedia.org
guangzhou0.29 USD 2026-05-07 zh.wikipedia.org4 lines 2026-06-08 (oldid 1358345659) en.wikipedia.org
mexico city0.29 USD 2025-12-26 infobae.com0 lines 2026-06-08 (oldid 1358345659) en.wikipedia.org
cairo0.19 USD 2026-03-27 egyptianstreets.com0 lines 2026-06-08 (oldid 1358345659) en.wikipedia.org
osaka1.19 USD 2026 en.wikipedia.org1 lines 2026-06-08 (oldid 1358345659) en.wikipedia.org
istanbul0.87 USD 2026 en.wikipedia.org4 lines 2026-06-08 (oldid 1358345659) en.wikipedia.org

Interchange is computed from the same snapshot as the other geometry stats; its per-city value is in the deck data and on each card. Driverless counts are from the driverless-train-systems list at the revision named in the rows.

The diagrams on the backs

The lore side of each card carries the diagram riders actually see, as a Wikimedia Commons recreation, credited on the card:

“Tokyo Subway Linemap” by Yveltal, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0

“Seoul Metropolitan Subway network map” by Satellizer, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0 (diagram dated 2023, future lines as then planned)

“Singapore MRT and LRT System Map” by Aforl, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 3.0

“Hong Kong Railway Route Map” by Sameboat, Wikimedia Commons, public domain

“Carte Métro de Paris” by Rigil, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY 3.0

“Shanghai Metro Linemap” by Yveltal, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0

“Beijing Subway System Map” by Painjet, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0

“London Underground Overground DLR Crossrail map” by Sameboat, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0

“NYC subway map” by CountZ (Jake Berman) & TFSyndicate, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 3.0 (the Tauranac-form diagram, the city’s map for 45 years)

“Madrid Metro Map” by Javitomad and contributors, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0

“Moscow metro map” by Sameboat & IKhitron, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0

“Copenhagen Metro 2024” by Tomtom24, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0

“Delhi Metro Network 2020” by AshuArtsNew, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0 (diagram dated 2020; Phase-4 openings not yet drawn)

“Guangzhou Metro Network” by Alan Fan Pei, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0

“Plano del Metro de la Ciudad de México” by ManuelContreras1996, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0 (diagram dated 2023, drawn to the sections then in service)

“Cairo Metro map” by Momooh, Wikimedia Commons, CC0

“Wide-Area Map of Osaka City Subway” by Excl-zoo, Wikimedia Commons, public domain (diagram c.2011 in the pre-2018 Municipal Subway idiom; the 2024 and 2025 extensions not drawn)

“Istanbul Railway Systems Network Map” by Alikam1896, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0

Official schematic artwork appears nowhere on this site: the major operators enforce copyright on their map artwork, so the long-maintained Commons recreations are the legal route to the familiar map. Every back’s file and licence was verified on its Commons page before it shipped.

stats © OpenStreetMap contributors · ODbL · OSM snapshot 2026-06-12 · lore diagrams credited on each card made by ajin.im
